body_txt: The Hippie Teacher eschews traditional academic discipline with a more laid-back, free spirited, peace, love and grooviness approach to their curriculum. While they may or may not be a good teacher, they will rarely (if ever) be a good disciplinarian. They'll often let students call them by their first name ("'Mr. Hippie' is my father; you can call me Rod") and a strange incense may be smelled from their office . Definitely Truth in Television ; while they can be found in different areas of the school they usually tend to be in liberal arts or other "soft" or "creative" subjects, or serving as guidance counselors . Hard sciences are less common, unless combined with Absent-Minded Professor . When portrayed sympathetically, this character is usually a Cool Teacher who's passionate about their students and finds alternative ways to influence the Book Dumb , while if portrayed unsympathetically, they'll either be an incompetent pushover trying to coast by with minimal effort and handing out A's like candy, or view their students as an entire generation they can program their own counterculture or anti-authority ideals on . May overlap with a Sadist Teacher , but more likely to be depicted as harmless or a positive influence. Foil to the Stern Teacher . Compare with Misplaced Kindergarten Teacher and Psychologist Teacher , Hippie Parents for the parent variant, and Loony Librarian .
